title: A Tree Grows In Springfield

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The familiar frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - high character integration and economy, but zero originality as it's been used thousands of times

jokes.text: Yes, we're at the point where baseball becomes mildly interesting.
jokes.setup: Setting up a climactic baseball moment
jokes.punchline: Undercutting with 'mildly interesting' - a dig at baseball's pace
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about baseball's reputation for being slow-paced, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: And apparently I'm married to a pork chop and have drumstick children. How did that happen?
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he's food in his dream
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact confusion about his nonsensical family situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with Homer's characteristic confused acceptance of bizarre circumstances

jokes.text: And the ball shatters the sky, bringing the ocean itself down into the stadium!
jokes.punchline: Escalates to reality-breaking proportions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane to impossible, typical of dream logic comedy

jokes.text: The groundskeepers are trying to put on the tarp, but it turned into a manta ray and stung them all.
jokes.punchline: Tarp becomes a dangerous sea creature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant surreal transformation of mundane object into threatening creature, perfectly executed

jokes.text: Now, we'll be right back after this word from oxygen, which Homer desperately needs to live.
jokes.punchline: Sponsorship by a basic biological necessity
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about basic human needs as commercial sponsors

jokes.text: Dad, all the jury summons you buried in the back yard are coming back up.
jokes.setup: Rain causing problems in the yard
jokes.punchline: Reveals Homer's illegal disposal of jury duty notices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - avoiding civic duty in the most ridiculous way possible. Great character integration and economy

jokes.text: Like one of them Charlie Brown wiggle-frowns.
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Peanuts animation style
jokes.explanation: Clever visual reference that works both as Barney's observation and meta-commentary on animation styles

jokes.text: I'm in an abusive relationship with life. It keeps beating the hell out of me, and I'm too cowardly to leave it.
jokes.punchline: Framing existence as an abusive relationship he can't escape
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor that's both funny and genuinely poignant. Perfect economy and structure

jokes.text: Maybe I could drink myself to death. Eh, well, you can't. Your tolerance is too high.
jokes.punchline: Barney's matter-of-fact assessment that Homer's too good at drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney response - treating Homer's drinking prowess as both compliment and obstacle to self-destruction

jokes.text: Life is too fragile. One minute you're lying in your hammock drinking beer, the next thing you know you're sittin' here drinkin' beer.
jokes.punchline: His example shows no actual change in his situation
jokes.explanation: Outstanding structure - sets up profound observation then reveals Homer's limited perspective. Perfect character moment

jokes.text: Ah, screw it. I quit. Janette, Cheryl, get your own ride home.
jokes.punchline: Gives up mid-sentence and abandons his strippers
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment showing his incompetence and casual mention of strippers

jokes.text: Okay, now hop on my imaginary motorcycle... arms around my belly...
jokes.setup: Wiggum offering to help Homer get home
jokes.punchline: Pretend transportation with uncomfortably intimate instructions
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's childlike solution creates awkward physical comedy scenario

jokes.text: Um, I'm gonna need you to chip in for gas. Argh. This is where the pretending ends.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum breaks character over imaginary gas money
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - even in fantasy, Wiggum's cheapness emerges. Great character consistency

jokes.text: The dog's got the mumps. Dogs can't get mumps. Well, then, explain this.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning if dogs can get mumps
jokes.punchline: Marge presents visual 'proof' that defies logic
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag setup with animal illness, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Willie, I love your chaps. Me pants are ripped out!
jokes.punchline: Willie clarifies they're torn pants, not leather chaps
jokes.explanation: Good Willie moment with his accent creating the misunderstanding about his 'chaps'

jokes.text: I don't care for silent auctions. It just encourages hovering.
jokes.punchline: Specific complaint about the social awkwardness they create
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about auction dynamics, but lacks character specificity

jokes.text: You know you just pushed it up to list price. You know that, right?
jokes.punchline: Pointing out they've defeated the purpose of an auction
jokes.explanation: Solid auction joke but generic and lacks character voice

jokes.text: Edna, I'm not one for costumes, but I sure do like that feather in your hair. I like how you make me feel good about the littlest things.
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly romantic and overly sincere public declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - awkward, overly formal romantic expression that creates secondhand embarrassment

jokes.text: It's just you and me tonight, Buttermilk.
jokes.punchline: Skinner has a pet name that's both childish and food-related
jokes.explanation: Skinner's awkward pet name for Edna fits his character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Skinner! People want to get home! Speak regular.
jokes.setup: Skinner speaking in Western dialect as emcee
jokes.punchline: Crowd's impatience with his affected persona
jokes.explanation: Good crowd reaction to Skinner's tendency to get carried away with themes

jokes.text: or a spaghetti dinner with the Van Houtens.
jokes.punchline: Dinner with the dysfunctional Van Houten family as clearly undesirable
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to established family dynamics, making their company an obviously bad prize

jokes.text: I could do my cyber-bullying on the bus!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's first thought is using it for harmful purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line - updates his bullying to modern technology while maintaining his character essence

jokes.text: Remember when me gettin' a cell phone was a big deal?
jokes.punchline: Reference to how quickly tech becomes obsolete
jokes.explanation: Meta observation about the show's own technology progression, mildly clever

jokes.text: Aw, that's Tooth Fairy money down the drain, sweetie.
jokes.setup: Lisa buying Homer a raffle ticket
jokes.punchline: Homer's pessimism and reference to Lisa's childish income source
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer negativity with sweet detail about Lisa's finances

jokes.text: I never win anything. It's always some stupid jerk you've never heard of.
jokes.punchline: Insults unknown winners while about to become one
jokes.explanation: Good setup for ironic reversal, shows Homer's resentment toward lucky people

jokes.text: Get him! I mean, woo-hoo!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's instinctive aggressive reaction followed by correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson moment - his bully instincts override social appropriateness before he catches himself

jokes.text: Three cheers for Homer, minus the one we gave him already.
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic precision in measuring enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner line showing his compulsive need for administrative accuracy even in celebration

jokes.text: Free Tibet! You heard me! Free him now!
jokes.setup: Homer on stage making political statement
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'Free Tibet' as liberating a person named Tibet
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding of political slogan, perfect character ignorance played for laughs

jokes.text: This is Homer Simpson's 13th raffle and first win.
jokes.punchline: Specific statistics highlighting his previous bad luck
jokes.explanation: Good statistical humor emphasizing Homer's streak of bad luck

jokes.text: I left a half-eaten apple outside your store... the greatest tribute of all.
jokes.setup: Steve Jobs parody introducing himself from beyond grave
jokes.punchline: Describes littering as ultimate honor
jokes.explanation: Good parody of Jobs' pretentious product presentations and Apple logo symbolism

jokes.text: The product you hold is like a giant expensive smartphone that can't call anyone-- it's that incredible.
jokes.punchline: Presents lack of phone capability as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Apple marketing spin - turning limitation into feature with perfect sarcasm

jokes.text: Or you could buy something from Hewlett-Packard.
jokes.setup: Pressuring user to agree to terms
jokes.punchline: Presents competitor as ultimate threat/punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect tech industry gatekeeping - HP as the boring alternative to cool Apple products

jokes.text: I submit! I submit!
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete capitulation to corporate pressure
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his susceptibility to authority and pressure

jokes.text: Ooh, that adult Etch-A-Sketch certainly has brightened your mood.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about his new tablet
jokes.punchline: Marge's dismissive description of high-tech device
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge skepticism about technology, reducing iPad to childhood toy

jokes.text: Virtual Stapler! Sounds just like a stapler, and it never runs out... unless you tell it to.
jokes.punchline: Enthusiasm for completely pointless simulation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of meaningless apps that simulate mundane real-world objects

jokes.text: I'm Sonny Bono. Watch out for that tree!
jokes.punchline: References Sonny Bono's death in skiing accident
jokes.explanation: Dark celebrity death humor typical of Simpsons, though somewhat cheap

jokes.text: I always thought the Pringles Man had the kindest eyes.
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet, naive observation about corporate mascot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge line - finding humanity and kindness in commercial imagery

jokes.text: Boy, you know, when you're just here alone, I can really smell ya.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest observation about Homer's hygiene
jokes.explanation: Typical Barney bluntness, though coming from him it's ironic given his own hygiene

jokes.text: It's like I'm skydiving with all my friends!
jokes.punchline: Overwrought description of video chat as extreme sport
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to overstate mundane experiences as epic adventures

jokes.text: So, uh, what are you pullin' the ripcord with?
jokes.punchline: Lenny playing along then pointing out logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Good Lenny moment - going along with Homer's fantasy then deflating it with logic

jokes.text: The old stupid moustache'll win him over. I'm really stupid.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to charm IRS auditor
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that his plan is dumb
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of self-awareness about his own stupidity

jokes.text: Unhand your Edison slate and bring your gold-brickery to a caesura!
jokes.setup: Burns yelling at Homer about the tablet
jokes.punchline: Archaic language for modern technology and work shirking
jokes.explanation: Outstanding Burns dialogue - perfect antiquated language applied to modern concepts. 'Edison slate' and 'caesura' are brilliant

jokes.text: Your remonstrances are a welcome boon and surely will redouble my diligence.
jokes.punchline: Homer matching Burns' archaic speech pattern perfectly
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to app influence on Homer - he's unconsciously adopted period language

jokes.text: Dad, I'm walking on my hands, and the lions think they're gonna eat me!
jokes.punchline: Even more dangerous situation still being ignored
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation showing Homer's technology addiction

jokes.text: What country is Mexico in?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to use location app
jokes.punchline: Fundamental geographical confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - geography question that reveals complete lack of basic knowledge

jokes.text: A paper-based read-a-majig? What are we, cavemen?
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissal of books as primitive technology
jokes.explanation: Good technology satire through Homer's newfound tech snobbery

jokes.text: What would you have me do? One basket for each egg?
jokes.setup: Marge warning about putting all eggs in one basket
jokes.punchline: Homer takes idiom literally and finds it absurd
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer literal interpretation of common saying, showing his concrete thinking

jokes.text: Ooh, we really have been off-a the mark.
jokes.setup: Learning how to make pizza from app
jokes.punchline: Italian chef admitting he's been doing pizza wrong
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Italian chef learning pizza from American app

jokes.text: Homer, you're gonna be fine, but there's no hope for the myPad.
jokes.punchline: More concern for device than patient, plus 'hope' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for 'hope' theme while showing tech priority over human welfare

jokes.text: It still had 88% of its charge.
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on irrelevant battery stat
jokes.explanation: Perfect modern tech grief - mourning the battery life rather than the device itself

jokes.text: Which of you is the YouTube of the kid high on dentist's gas?
jokes.setup: Doctor trying to identify viral video patient
jokes.punchline: Using 'YouTube' as a verb while describing embarrassing medical moment
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about viral medical content and language evolution

jokes.text: knowing full well I had declined thy infernal Mapple Care.
jokes.setup: Homer praying about his broken tablet
jokes.punchline: Religious language applied to tech warranty
jokes.explanation: Excellent blend of religious language with modern tech concerns - 'thy infernal Mapple Care' is brilliant

jokes.text: Keep your pants on, Flanders. Oh, I will. Till I go to bed and take them off under the covers.
jokes.setup: Homer telling Ned to stay calm
jokes.punchline: Ned's overshare about his private bedtime routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders TMI moment - innocent but uncomfortable personal detail

jokes.text: Ooh, look at me, I turn sunlight into complex sugars. Big whoop.
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically describes photosynthesis as unimpressive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant scientific literacy joke - reducing the miracle of photosynthesis to sarcastic dismissal

jokes.text: Is this one of those coincidences like Jesus on a tortilla, or George Washington on a dollar bill?
jokes.setup: Homer questioning if the tree message is a miracle
jokes.punchline: Compares religious apparition to printed currency design
jokes.explanation: Outstanding joke equating supernatural phenomena with intentional design. Perfect logic and structure

jokes.text: somebody up there is trying to tell you that whatever flat tires you've got in your life, God's there with his little spare and it enables you to drive real slow till you get to the next station.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate car metaphor that becomes oddly specific and limiting
jokes.explanation: Typical Ned over-explanation with car metaphor that gets too detailed and loses impact

jokes.text: Fine, I'll just go eat the body of God. That's not crazy.
jokes.setup: Ned telling Homer not to eat the tree
jokes.punchline: Homer points out communion as equally bizarre when described literally
jokes.explanation: Excellent religious satire - Homer's literal description of communion highlights its absurdity

jokes.text: Duff Adequate.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for Moe's finest beer
jokes.punchline: Brand name that sets extremely low expectations
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy joke with brand name that's hilariously honest about quality

jokes.text: Wow, no one's ever been happy in this place before.
jokes.punchline: Moe's matter-of-fact observation about his establishment's depressing nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe self-awareness about his bar being a place of misery

jokes.text: And when I started it was Moe's Ice Cream Carnival-- not good.
jokes.setup: Talking about his bar's depressing atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Reveals previous business was also unsuccessful
jokes.explanation: Good Moe backstory showing his consistent business failures

jokes.text: Huckleberry, Overbite, Picklefoot, Edumacation. Oh, it says hope!
jokes.setup: Cletus trying to spell out the word on tree
jokes.punchline: His phonetic spelling system using nonsense words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Cletus moment - his illiteracy creates an elaborate phonetic system that's both stupid and clever

jokes.text: Just like in Hope Floats, that Sandra Bullock movie I wrote.
jokes.punchline: Claims to have written famous Hollywood movie
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus delusion - the rural character claiming Hollywood writing credit

jokes.text: Now, stay tuned for your lucky lotto numbers. It's your turn for sure.
jokes.punchline: Immediately promotes equally superstitious lottery belief
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy joke - Kent mocking superstition while immediately promoting lottery gambling

jokes.text: I'll take your money. The tree's a fraud. I just got a hundred. The tree is real!
jokes.setup: Kent offering money for truth about tree
jokes.punchline: Person immediately changes their story based on payment
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of how money influences 'truth' in journalism and witness testimony

jokes.text: I'm on a break, kid, and when I'm on a break, the mouse is dead. The mouse was a man. His teeth were yellow. His breath was rancid.
jokes.punchline: Actor breaks character to reveal disturbing reality behind the magic
jokes.explanation: Dark Disney satire destroying childhood innocence with harsh reality of costume performers

jokes.text: The castle is plywood?!
jokes.punchline: Child reporter's innocence being shattered by construction details
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to disillusionment theme - even architecture disappoints

jokes.text: Teeny's a girl? Oh, wait, he's just a bottom.
jokes.punchline: Clarification using circus hierarchy that sounds like sexual position
jokes.explanation: Weak innuendo humor relying on double meaning without much craft

jokes.text: Thank God there's no alcohol in this bar, or this place would really go up.
jokes.setup: Crowd wanting to burn things in anger
jokes.punchline: Moe grateful his bar has no alcohol to fuel fire
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moe moment - finding silver lining in his business being terrible

jokes.text: Is that a 212?
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical concern about area code for Hell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating supernatural as mundane phone call with area code concerns

jokes.text: I thought your hair was just blue cotton candy, but now I know it's a solid loaf of brain.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre food metaphors for both appearance and intelligence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer romantic moment using his characteristic food-based metaphors

jokes.text: I thought your hair was just blue cotton candy, but now I know it's a solid loaf of brain.
jokes.punchline: Describing intelligence through food metaphors in Homer-specific way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - expressing love through food metaphors with backhanded compliment about her hair

jokes.text: Homie, you have such a way with foods.
jokes.punchline: Marge's gentle correction and acceptance of his limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - lovingly accepting Homer's weird communication style

jokes.text: But I just got this three months ago. Yeah, that's the kind of thing I do, and yet I still wound up here, as your boss.
jokes.punchline: Steve Jobs admitting exploitative business model while highlighting his success
jokes.explanation: Good tech industry satire about planned obsolescence and corporate success despite exploitation

jokes.text: Can I get Christmas off? It's my kid's birthday.
jokes.punchline: Confused about which kid or conflating holidays with birthdays
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer confusion about family details and holidays

tmdb_overview: Homer wins a MyPad at the school auction and Steve Mobbs, who is now in heaven, talks to Homer through it. Homer goes into a funk when he breaks the myPad, until Ned Flanders discovers a miracle tree in the Simpsons' back garden.
